title: Documentation, Change, Lifecycle, and Monitoring · Spiral Review
summary: Operate a living network source of truth with baselines, telemetry, alerts, configuration, and service ownership. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an operations and observability runbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain physical, logical, rack, cable, Layer 1 to 3 diagrams, asset inventory, licensing, warranty, and IPAM records.
Use SLAs, service objectives, wireless surveys, and heat maps with date, method, owner, and limitation context.
Plan EOL, EOS, patches, operating systems, firmware, licensing, replacement, and secure decommissioning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Net Diagrams Inventory Ipam evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Net Diagrams Inventory Ipam assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Maintain physical, logical, rack, cable, Layer 1 to 3 diagrams, asset inventory, licensing, warranty, and IPAM records. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network operations monitoring. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Diagrams Inventory Ipam, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of an operations and observability runbook while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: One automatically discovered topology replaces every physical and ownership record. This fails the Net Diagrams Inventory Ipam contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Zero Trust Architecture (NIST SP 800-207) — Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain physical, logical, rack, cable, Layer 1 to 3 diagrams, asset inventory, licensing, warranty, and IPAM records.

hints: Name the Net Diagrams Inventory Ipam precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network operations monitoring. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One automatically discovered topology replaces every physical and ownership record.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Discovery Mirroring Monitoring applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use scheduled or ad hoc discovery, port mirroring, and traffic, performance, availability, and configuration monitoring.
